real_insight: The pain of pausing a slipping migration is not symmetric across colleagues. The engineers who already wished you would fail are not just disappointed peers — they are an active narrating force in the architecture-review channel, and the slip will become THEIR story unless you finish. The verse names this dynamic as part of the cost calculation, not as ego.
objection: Could be misread as 'never pause a project because critics will mock you' — that would be fortune-cookie. The falsifiability and counter-example explicitly defend against this misreading by anchoring to Ch. 18.66's right-renunciation: when the technical case for pausing is sound, the pain of the rewriting is real but does not invert the call. The engineering layer is operationally specific (the bypassed reviewer, the architect whose design was retired, the staff engineer who watched the bypass) and names a real failure mode that senior engineers face.
